message:
perf(tools): optimize ReadTool line formatting to avoid intermediate allocations

Replace the collect-into-Vec-then-join pattern in ReadTool's line
formatting with a single pre-allocated String and direct write!() calls.
This eliminates one Vec<String> allocation plus the join() copy for every
file read operation.

Additional micro-optimizations:
- Use into_owned() instead of to_string() on Cow<str> from
  String::from_utf8_lossy to avoid a redundant allocation when the
  content is already valid UTF-8
- Move truncation.content directly into output_text instead of cloning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
